data.title: Trader Eugene: ETH has been removed from the portfolio, SOL key support is at $80, BTC is expected to be supported in the $74-76K range
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News: Well-known trader Eugene shared his market views on the X platform. He said that SOL is a good mean reversion buy point around $80, but if it falls below that level, the next support may be as far as $25.
For BTC, Eugene expects the $74K-$76K range to provide good support, and assuming Michael Saylor does not sell his holdings, BTC is expected to be stable at $52K. But if Saylor sells massively, $25K may become a new buy point.
In addition, he revealed that he has removed ETH from investable assets.

data.title: Gate.io launches OBT perpetual contract, supporting up to 50x leverage
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Gate.io has launched OBT perpetual contract real-time trading (USDT settlement), supporting 1-50 times leverage.

data.title: Planet Lunch News
Russia uses cryptocurrency in oil trade with India to circumvent Western sanctions
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to four people familiar with the matter, Russia is using cryptocurrencies to settle oil trades with India to circumvent Western sanctions. This practice marks Russia's further expansion of the application of digital currencies in international trade. Although Russia has legislated last year to allow the use of cryptocurrency payments in international trade, there have been no previous reports that it has been actually used in oil trade. Sources said that some Russian oil companies are using crypto assets such as Bitcoin (BTC), Ethereum (ETH) and Tether (USDT) to facilitate the exchange between Indian rupees and Russian rubles. According to the International Energy Agency (IEA), Russia's total oil trade in 2023 will reach US$192 billion, of which cryptocurrency transactions still account for a small proportion, but show a growing trend. The encrypted payment model has previously been adopted by sanctioned countries such as Iran and Venezuela to circumvent the dominance of the US dollar in the global oil market. (Jinshi)

data.title: LBank will launch pre-market trading of Abstract (ABS)
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News LBank announcement shows that LBank will launch Abstract (ABS) pre-market trading at 14:00 (SGT) on March 14, and open ABS/USDT trading pairs. It is reported that the delivery of ABS pre-market trading will be carried out at the official opening. LBank will redeem tokens 1:1 for ABS pre-market trading and official trading, and promise that the withdrawal time of ABS will not be later than other centralized exchanges in the market.
At the same time, in order to protect the interests of users to the greatest extent, LBank launched a price protection limited activity to provide all-round protection for ABS spot pre-market trading. When the pre-market trading ends, if the price of ABS held by the user is lower than the average pre-market trading price of the industry market, the loss caused by the price drop will be subsidized by LBank, up to 50USDT, and the subsidy will be issued to the user's spot account in the form of USDT within 3 days after the end of the pre-market trading.

data.title: Arkansas Crypto Association sues state officials, challenging law targeting foreign miners
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News The Arkansas Crypto Mining Association filed a lawsuit against state Attorney General Tim Griffin and Oil and Gas Commission Director Lawrence Bengal in the U.S. District Court for the Eastern District on March 13, accusing them of enforcing unconstitutional and discriminatory state regulations that prohibit foreign-born U.S. citizens from engaging in crypto mining activities. Previously, a federal court temporarily prohibited Arkansas from preventing a naturalized Chinese American citizen from operating a crypto mining business in November last year. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: Bithumb will launch OBT Korean Won trading pair
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: According to the official announcement, Bithumb will launch the Orbiter Finance (OBT) Korean won trading pair.

data.title: Delaware Court Grants Temporary Relief in Bitcoin Mining Dispute
data.description:
According to Cointelegraph, a Delaware court has issued a temporary restraining order in favor of Consensus Colocation, a Bitcoin mining firm from Pennsylvania, in its ongoing payment dispute with hosting provider Mawson Infrastructure Group. The court's decision prevents Mawson from blocking access to or using the 21,000 mining rigs owned by Consensus at the Midland, Pennsylvania facility.

Vice Chancellor Morgan Zurn granted the order on March 12, following a request from Consensus Colocation and its systems owner, Stone Ridge Ventures. The dispute centers around alleged unpaid fees, the terms of their agreement, and Consensus's plans to relocate. Consensus claims that Mawson has been operating the rigs for its own benefit since February 28, after blocking Consensus's personnel from accessing the site. Mawson, however, argues that their agreement allows them to use the rigs and grants them the right of first refusal regarding relocation plans.

The restraining order prohibits Mawson from utilizing the hashrate generated by the miners and restricts them from limiting Consensus's digital and physical access to the equipment. This order will remain in effect until a preliminary injunction hearing can be held. Mawson Infrastructure Group and Consensus Colocation have not yet responded to requests for comment.

The legal conflict began with a complaint filed on March 6, where Consensus accused Mawson of using their Bitcoin mining rigs, valued at $30 million, to generate daily profits between $100,000 and $200,000 while denying Consensus access both physically and through VPN. The two companies had entered into a colocation agreement in December 2023, which was set to end by March 2025, with a phased reduction in capacity and a removal process starting on March 3.

Mawson contends that it is owed fees and electricity prepayments for February and March, and claims that the colocation agreement permits them to redirect the hashrate to cover these costs. However, Consensus's legal representatives argue that the agreement's terms were only applicable before April 1, 2024, and under specific conditions related to deposit replenishment. They assert that Mawson's actions have resulted in the unauthorized use of hashrate worth significantly more than the $17,505.45 Mawson claims is owed in late fees.

data.title: Bitcoin Symbol Featured in Austin Drone Show
data.description: According to Foresight News, a drone show in Austin, Texas, showcased a formation of the Bitcoin symbol. The event highlighted the growing influence and recognition of cryptocurrency in various cultural and technological displays.
